Python PCA on correlation matrix of extracted data yields eigenvalue sum = 6.0 (within range 5.9-6.1). For PCA on correlation matrix of p variables, eigenvalue sum = p by mathematical property (sum of diagonal = trace = p).

privileged_info_summary: The wage_survey dataset is a synthetic 60x6 cross-sectional dataset embedded in setup_task.sh. Variables: wage (hourly wage in dollars), educ (years of education), exper (years of work experience), age (age in years), hrswk (usual hours worked per week), tenure (years with current employer). PCA on this data's correlation matrix yields an eigenvalue sum of exactly 6.0 (for 6 variables), which is within the specified range [5.9, 6.1]. The task requires saving three output files: pca_analysis.inp (Gretl script), pca_results.txt (PCA text output), and wage_survey_pca.gdt (augmented dataset with principal component scores).
